summary:
Every one of the four is short now. MNQ never threatened yesterday's flipped stop and spent the session grinding down through the 29,633 AVWAP, the 29,500 psych and the -2s band to a 29,079 low, closing -1.78%. MES lost 7,579.57 and flipped, which retires the rotation story: this was broad de-risking, and yesterday's map named that exact level as the tell. Gold was the loudest print, -2.13% and through 4,000, with the stop flipping short. Crude held 78.59 on every close and flipped anyway, because the stop trailed down to price rather than price trading down to the stop.

What makes today worth studying is that the data was good. Retail sales landed in line, jobless claims beat, Philly Fed came in roughly four times consensus. Strong data pushed yields up, and long-duration tech paid for it. Gold losing 4,000 on the same print is the confirmation: this was a rate repricing, and both the growth and haven trades sat on the wrong side of it.

event.name: Retail Sales (June), Initial Jobless Claims, Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing (July)
event.consensus: Retail sales +0.2% MoM; claims 217,000; Philly Fed roughly 10
event.actual: Retail sales +0.2% MoM (May revised up to +1.0%, ex-gas +0.7%, +6.72% YoY); claims 208,000; Philly Fed 41.4
event.surprise: STRONG. Retail sales in line, claims well below forecast, Philly Fed roughly 4x consensus
event.nqReaction: Inverted. MNQ was already leaking before the 12:30 UTC release, losing 29,530 on the 11:00 UTC bar. The 12:00 UTC bar closed 29,392 and the sell-off extended all session to a 29,079 RTH low, closing 29,178.50 (-1.78%). No bounce on good data.
event.crossAsset: Gold fell 2.13% and lost 4,000 on the same release, which is what happens when strong data cuts rate-cut odds. Yields rose and long-duration tech de-rated with them. MES lost its stop for the first time in the sequence, so weakness broadened past tech. Reporting also points to a semiconductor sell-off on AI capex skepticism, with Taiwan Semiconductor lower despite beating on earnings and guiding strong. Good news raised the discount rate, and both the growth trade and the haven trade were on the wrong side.

observe.name: ES/NQ confirmation
observe.note: Yesterday this split and was logged BROKEN, MES long while MNQ was short. Today they agree again, both short. The confirmation reformed on the bearish side, the outcome yesterday's map flagged as the bigger tell.

risk.ddProximity: Not binding. The book entered flat and the full daily budget is intact.
risk.sizing: The MNQ RTH range was 430.00 points against a 126.28 ATR. Volatility expanded, so contract count comes down to hold constant dollar risk. Same risk, smaller size.
risk.openRisk: Four correlated shorts is one bet wearing four costumes. MNQ, MES, MGC and MCL are all short on the same rate-repricing driver, so treat the stack as one position for exposure purposes and size the basket, not the legs.
risk.note: The direction is aligned and that argues for a lighter throttle than a split tape. Pulling the other way: three of the four flips are one session old, and a fresh flip is the least reliable state a trend stop has. MGC has whipsawed twice in three sessions. MCL flipped without ever losing 78.59 on a close, meaning the stop caught up to price rather than price breaking down, which is a weaker signal. Week ahead: Friday July 17 is monthly options expiration, which can pin or unpin things for reasons unrelated to the macro read, alongside Housing Starts, Industrial Production and preliminary UMich. REDUCED stands.

psychology:
Yesterday's map handed you a line and said, in writing, that MES losing 7,580 would change the read. Today MES closed 7,569. The useful question is not whether you saw it, it is whether you acted on it while it was happening or waited for the close to make it official.

Most traders write a condition down and then negotiate with it in real time. Price gets to the level, the level does its job, and the trader decides that this particular touch does not count. That is not analysis, it is the reluctance to be wrong about the previous read. The whole point of writing a level down the night before is that your calmer self already decided what it means. Honor the note or change your process, but do not do the thing where you keep the plan and ignore the trigger.

fullReport.title: Strategy Habitat Check
fullReport.html:
StrategyHabitatRead
TSMOM-20 (1hr trend momentum)IN_HABITATThe cleanest state it has had in a week. Direction is trending and all four stops agree short. MNQ 315.71 under its stop, MES 44.08 under, MGC 45.20 under, MCL 1.04 under.
AVWAP Bounce (continuation off AVWAP)BORDERLINEAVWAP flipped from support to overhead resistance at 29,289.55 and price closed 111.05 below it. Structure is right for bounce-shorts, but there has been no retest from below yet, so the setup is anticipated rather than present.
AVWAP Hand-off (short only, OR break to AVWAP)BORDERLINEDirection and short-only bias fit. The MNQ OR broke down at 17:40 UTC (13:40 ET) and never reclaimed, but that break landed inside the HOF 12:00 to 14:00 ET dead zone, so the filter excluded it. Right regime, wrong clock.
AVWAP Deviation Band (reversion at +/-2s, +/-3s)BORDERLINEThe -2s tagged at the 29,079 RTH low (-2.19s) and price closed back at -1.15s, technically a reversion. Trend days are hostile ground for a reversion edge, so a band tag inside an expanding downtrend is low-conviction.

Out of habitat today: AVWAP Midrange (no range; the session trended), AVWAP Back Into Range (MNQ broke down and never closed back inside the OR), E3v2 Momentum Long (long-only; the regime filter blocks it in a confirmed downtrend).

fullReport.title: NY Opening Range / COMPASS
fullReport.html:
SymbolOR HighOR LowWidthBreakTime (UTC)PriceClosed back inside
MNQ29,532.0029,257.75274.25DOWN17:4029,243.75No
MES7,604.007,575.2528.75UP14:157,608.00No, reversed through the OR low
MGC4,012.703,985.5027.20UP15:254,015.60No, reversed through the OR low
MCL80.3779.700.67DOWN14:5079.64No

The MNQ opening range was 274.25 points wide, roughly 2.2x its ATR(14). A range that wide says the open had no agreement in it, and price stayed inside for over four hours before resolving down. MES and MGC both broke up first and then reversed all the way through their OR lows, the failed-breakout signature: the initial break is the trap and the reversal carried the information.

fullReport.title: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter)
fullReport.html: No long signal. E3v2 Momentum Long requires a regime-filter pass, and the Direction axis is a confirmed downtrend with all four stops short. The filter is doing its job here: the setup is blocked precisely on the day the underlying breakout would have failed.

fullReport.title: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ)
fullReport.html:
BandLevelTouched
+3s29,578.52No
+2s29,482.19No
+1s29,385.87Yes, early RTH
AVWAP29,289.55Yes
-1s29,193.23Yes
-2s29,096.90Yes, RTH low tagged -2.19s
-3s29,000.58No

The -2s tagged at the 29,079 low and price closed back at -1.15s, so the band produced a technical reversion. Read it with care: sigma expanded from 67.95 to 96.32 today, so the bands themselves widened as the trend ran. A band stretching to keep up with price is describing a trend, not a range.

1MNQ stays below the 29,836.40 flipped-short stop on hourly closes: short flip stands, 29,633 AVWAP then 29,500 psych and the 29,497 -2s are the downside referencesTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUTHighest session close 29,744, never near 29,836. All three references reached: 29,633 AVWAP taken out on the 07:00 UTC bar, 29,500 psych lost by 11:00 UTC, 29,497 -2s cleared. RTH low 29,079.
2MES holds above its 7,579.57 stop and its 7,605 AVWAP while MNQ stays under 29,836: rotation read stays intactNOT TRIGGEREDn/aMES did not hold. Closed 7,569, under the 7,579.57 stop, and flipped short. The row's stated alternate ('if MES also loses 7,580 the read changes from rotation to broad de-risking, which is the bigger tell') is what happened.
3MNQ reclaims 29,836 and the 29,892.25 RTH high on hourly closes: one-session shakeout, 30,000 and the 30,062.5 overnight high back in playNOT TRIGGEREDn/aNo hourly close above 29,836 at any point. Session high 29,796.50, set on the 05:00 UTC bar.
4MGC holds the 4,033.58 stop and the 4,061 AVWAP: chop toward the 4,089 RTH high and the 4,100 psych continuesNOT TRIGGEREDn/aMGC did not hold. The stated alternate ('a 2-close below 4,033.6 reopens 4,000') fired on the 12:00 and 13:00 UTC bars at 3,996.5 and 3,994.3, and 4,000 gave way. RTH low 3,973.70.
5MCL holds above the 78.59 stop: the 80.70 RTH high and the 80.94 overnight high are the retest referencesTRIGGEREDMIXEDHeld on every RTH close, lowest close 78.76, though the 78.56 RTH low pierced it intraday by 0.03. Overnight ran to 80.87, within 0.07 of the 80.94 reference. RTH high only 80.31, short of 80.70, and the day closed -0.85% with the stop flipping short.

Score: 5 rows, 2 triggered, 1 played out, 0 failed. MTD: 35 triggered, 19 played out, 4 failed.

Filters Friday: no CPI, no FOMC, not Monday, not Wednesday. All four COMPASS playbooks eligible (BIR, HOF, MID, BNC). HOF dead zone 12:00 to 14:00 ET still applies; MID remains 9:30 to 10:30 ET only.

Calendar: Friday July 17, Import/Export Prices and Housing Starts 8:30 AM ET, Industrial Production 9:15 AM ET, preliminary University of Michigan sentiment 10:00 AM ET. Friday is also July monthly options expiration, so treat the open with the usual expiration-day caution around pinning.

yesterdaysMapScore.note: Graded the Jul 15 map (targeting Jul 16). Row 1 TRIGGERED / PLAYED OUT: MNQ never closed near 29,836 and all three downside references were reached to a 29,079 low. Row 5 TRIGGERED / MIXED: MCL held 78.59 on every RTH close (lowest 78.76) and the overnight ran to 80.87 within 0.07 of the 80.94 reference, but the RTH high stalled at 80.31 and the stop flipped short. Rows 2, 3 and 4 NOT TRIGGERED, though the stated alternates in rows 2 and 4 both fired and were accurate: MES lost 7,580 and turned rotation into broad de-risking, and MGC 2-closed under 4,033.6 and reopened 4,000.
